revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:皇室祭祀令]]
{{Japanese law | title = Imperial Household Ritual Ordinance | number = Imperial Household Ordinance No. 1 of September 19, 1908 | alias = | status = Abolished | type = {{ill|Constitutional law|en|Constitutional law|qid=Q11206|lt=Constitution|1=Constitutional law}} | content = {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=Imperial Household|1=Imperial House of Japan}} {{ill|Rituals|ja|祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q132241}} | related = | link = }} {{Imperial Household Rituals}}
'''Imperial Household Ritual Ordinance''' (皇室祭祀令, Kōshitsu Saishi Rei) was a {{ill|Law|ja|法令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Law|1=regulations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q106947327}} concerning {{ill|Rituals|ja|祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q132241}} of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=Imperial Household|1=Imperial House of Japan}}. It was promulgated as {{ill|Imperial Household Ordinance|ja|皇室令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Ordinance|1=Imperial Household Ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581032}} No. 1 in 1908 during the Meiji period and abolished on May 2, 1947, by {{ill|Imperial Household Ordinance No. 12 of 1947|ja|皇室令及附屬法令廢止ノ件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Ordinance No. 12 of 1947|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Despite its abolition, {{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|qid=Q11453226|lt=Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace|1=Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace}} continue to follow the provisions of the Imperial Household Ritual Ordinance.
== Overview ==
The ordinance consisted of three chapters: Chapter 1 (General Provisions), Chapter 2 (Great Rites), and Chapter 3 (Minor Rites).
The ritual format was outlined in the appendix of the ordinance, with Part 1 covering Great Rite Formats and Part 2 covering Minor Rite Formats.
Article 10 stipulated ceremonial anniversaries at intervals of 3, 5, 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 100 years, and every 100 years thereafter following a sovereign's passing. Other provisions included rituals during mourning periods (Articles 4 and 5), practices related to purification (Article 6), and rites involving tombs, official shrines, and other facilities (Articles 7, 23). Specific distinctions were also made regarding rituals held at the {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro|ja|賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|2=ja|3=賢所|4=fr|5=Kashiko-dokoro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōrei-den|ja|皇霊殿|lt=Kōrei-den (Imperial Ancestral Shrines)|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrei-den|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮中三殿}}, {{ill|Shinden|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|lt=Shinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinden|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} ([[Three Palace Sanctuaries]]), and other sites (Articles 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 18, 23, 24, 25).
=== Great Rites ===
Great Rites were performed personally by the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}}, accompanied by the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581068}} and {{ill|Bureaucrat|en|Bureaucrat|qid=Q572700|lt=Officials|1=Bureaucrat}}, or were conducted in a similar manner. They involved three days of purification, the wearing of ceremonial attire, and the presentation of the sacred imperial regalia during appearances.
* {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} - January 3 * {{ill|Kigensetsu Festival|ja|紀元節祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - February 11 * {{ill|Spring Koryosai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Spring Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} - Vernal Equinox * Spring Shrine Festival - Vernal Equinox * {{ill|Emperor Jimmu Festival|ja|神武天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu Emperor Festival|qq=}} - April 3 * {{ill|Autumn Koryosai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumn Koryosai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} - Autumnal Equinox * Autumn Shrine Festival - Autumnal Equinox * {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|qid=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} - October 17 * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} - November 23–24 * {{ill|Previous Emperor Festival|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Previous Emperor Festival|qq=}} - Anniversary of previous emperors' deaths * Decennial Rites for the three preceding sovereigns - Anniversaries of their passing * Decennial Rites for previous empresses - Anniversaries of their passing * Decennial Rites for imperial consorts - Anniversaries of their passing
Additionally, Great Rites were held for significant imperial or national occasions, including announcements to the {{ill|List of Jingū|en|List of Jingū|qid=Q120680118|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=List of Jingū}}, construction-related rituals, and the enshrinement or relocation of imperial spirits.
=== Minor Rites ===
Minor Rites were led by the {{ill|Shōtenchō|ja|掌典長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtenchō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 掌典職|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11498167}} or conducted similarly, with the Emperor performing worship alongside the Imperial Family and officials. These rites involved purification, ceremonial attire, and presentation of sacred regalia during appearances.
* {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} - January 1 * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai|1=Kinensai}} - February 17 * Kashiko-dokoro Mikagura - Mid-December * {{ill|Tenchōsetsu Festival|ja|天長節祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchōsetsu Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Emperor's Birthday * Annual Rites for the three preceding sovereigns - Anniversaries of their passing * Annual Rites for preceding empresses - Anniversaries of their passing * Annual Rites for imperial consorts - Anniversaries of their passing * Decennial Rites for the sovereigns from Emperor Suizei onward - Anniversaries of their passing
Other Minor Rites included rituals for enshrining spirits of consorts, crown princes, and other members of the Imperial Family into the {{ill|Kōrei-den|ja|皇霊殿|lt=Kōrei-den (Imperial Ancestral Shrines)|1=Three Palace Sanctuaries|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrei-den|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Palace Sanctuaries|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮中三殿}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Regarding Imperial Household Rituals|ja|皇室ノ祭祀ニ関スル件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regarding Imperial Household Rituals|1=Matters Concerning the Rituals of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109595717}}
{{Law-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|皇室祭祀令|version=85868780|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:皇室祭祀令]]
<!--Imperial Household Rites Act--> [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== {{Japanese Legal Instrument | Title = Imperial Household Ritual Ordinance | Number = {{ill|Imperial Household Order No. 1, September 19, Meiji 41|ja|明治41st year 9月19日皇室令第1号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Order No. 1, September 19, Meiji 41|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Common name = | Effect = Abolished | Type = Constitution | Content = Rituals of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=Imperial Household|1=Imperial House of Japan}} | Related = | Link = }}
{{Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace}} '''Imperial Household Ritual Ordinance''' ({{ill|こうしつさいしれい|ja|皇室祭祀令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/こうしつさいしれい|1=Imperial Household Rites Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581038}}) is a legal instrument concerning the rituals ({{ill|Rituals|ja|祭祀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rituals|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q132241}}) of the {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=Imperial Household|1=Imperial House of Japan}}. It was promulgated as {{ill|Imperial Household Order|ja|皇室令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Order|1=Imperial Household Ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581032}} No. 1 in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 41. It was abolished on May 2, 1947 ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}} 22) by the Imperial Household Order Abolishment ({{ill|Imperial Household Order|ja|皇室令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Order|1=Imperial Household Ordinance|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581032}} No. 12, Showa 22). Even after its abolition, the court ceremonies ({{ill|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|en|Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace|qid=Q11453226|lt=Ceremonies of the Japanese Imperial Palace|1=Ritual ceremonies of the Imperial Palace}}) continue to be conducted in accordance with this ordinance.
== Overview == The ordinance is structured into three chapters: the first chapter covers general provisions, the second chapter covers major festivals, and the third chapter covers minor festivals.
The ritual forms are set out in the attached forms, with the first part detailing the rites for the major festivals and the second part detailing those for the minor festivals.
The intervals for the regular annual ceremonies ({{ill|Shikinen-sai|ja|式年祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinen-sai|qq=}}) are prescribed in Article 10 as 3, 5, 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, and 100 years, and every 100 years thereafter from the day of an emperor’s {{ill|Demise of the Crown|en|Demise of the Crown|qid=Q842994|lt=Imperial Demise|1=Demise of the Crown}}. In addition, provisions are made for mourning rituals (Articles 4 and 5), for matters concerning purification (Article 6), for issues relating to imperial tombs and the offering of national and shrine coins (Articles 7 and 23), and for various other ceremonies held at shrines, the imperial spirit hall, shrine buildings, and other locations (Articles 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 18, 23, 24, and 25).
=== Major Festivals === Major Festivals ({{ill|大祭|ja|大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/大祭|1=Grand Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11438341}}) are ceremonies in which the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}}, accompanied by the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581068}} and {{ill|Bureaucrat|en|Bureaucrat|qid=Q572700|lt=Bureaucrats|1=Bureaucrat}}, personally presides over the rites (known as the {{ill|Emperor’s Personal Festival|ja|天皇親祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor’s Personal Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), or ceremonies of equivalent Rank. These ceremonies involve a three‐day period of ritual purification, the wearing of the highest ceremonial attire, and, when the emperor makes a public appearance, the bestowal of the imperial sword seal.
* {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} – January 3 * {{ill|Kigensetsu-sai|ja|紀元節祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigensetsu-sai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – February 11 * {{ill|Shunki Kōrei-sai|ja|春季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Spring Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shunki Kōrei-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} – on the Vernal Equinox Day * {{ill|Vernal Shrine Festival|ja|春季神殿祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vernal Shrine Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – on the Vernal Equinox Day * {{ill|Jinmu Tennō-sai|ja|神武天皇祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmu Tennō-sai|1=Emperor Jimmu Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590440}} – April 3 * {{ill|Shūki Kōrei-sai|ja|秋季皇霊祭|1=Kōreisai|lt=Autumn Kōreisai|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shūki Kōrei-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōreisai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇霊祭}} – on the Autumnal Equinox Day * {{ill|Autumnal Shrine Festival|ja|秋季神殿祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Autumnal Shrine Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – on the Autumnal Equinox Day * {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|qid=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}} – October 17 * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} – spanning November 23–24 * {{ill|Sentei-sai|ja|先帝祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sentei-sai|1=Previous Emperor Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11388139}} – on the day corresponding to an emperor’s demise * Regular ceremonies for the three previous generations of emperors ({{ill|Shikinen-sai|ja|式年祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinen-sai|qq=}}) – on the day corresponding to their demise * Successive regular ceremonies – on the day corresponding to an emperor’s demise * Regular ceremonies for the empress ({{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Empress consort|qid=Q7723211}}) – on the day corresponding to her demise
Furthermore, when matters of great national or imperial importance occur—such as the construction of a new shrine, the transfer of the enshrined spirit to a new shrine through construction, or the enshrinement of the spirits of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}}, {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Empress consort|qid=Q7723211}}, and other senior members in the imperial spirit hall—these ceremonies are conducted in accordance with the major festival rites.
=== Minor Festivals === Minor Festivals ({{ill|小祭|ja|小祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/小祭|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) are ceremonies in which the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}}, accompanied by the {{ill|Imperial Family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581068}} and {{ill|Bureaucrat|en|Bureaucrat|qid=Q572700|lt=Bureaucrats|1=Bureaucrat}}, personally offers worship, or ceremonies of equivalent Rank in which the {{ill|Chief Ritual Officer|ja|掌典長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chief Ritual Officer|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 掌典職|1=Chief Cleric of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11498167}} presides. These ceremonies require the observance of ritual purity on the day, the wearing of the highest ceremonial attire, and, when the emperor makes a public appearance, the bestowal of the imperial sword seal.
* {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} – January 1 * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Kinen-sai|1=Kinensai}} – February 17 * Sacred kagura at the shrine precincts – in mid-December * {{ill|Tenchō-sessai|ja|天長節祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sessai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – on the day corresponding to the emperor’s birthday * Annual ceremonies for the three previous generations of emperors – on the day corresponding to their demise * Successive annual ceremonies – on the day corresponding to an emperor’s demise * Annual ceremonies for the empress ({{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Empress consort|qid=Q7723211}}) – on the day corresponding to her demise * Regular annual ceremonies for the successive emperors from the reign of {{ill|Ten'yō|en|Ten'yō|qid=Q1194175|lt=Suijō Tennō|1=Ten'yō}} onward through four generations – on the day corresponding to their demise
Furthermore, when the enshrinement of the spirits of the {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Empress consort|qid=Q7723211}}, {{ill|Crown prince|en|Crown prince|qid=Q207293|lt=Crown Prince|1=Crown prince}}, {{ill|Crown Princess|ja|皇太子妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Princess|1=Crown Princess of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q986305}}, imperial grandchildren, imperial granddaughters, {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|qq=}}, {{ill|Princess Consort|ja|親王妃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Consort|1=princess|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5361359}}, {{ill|Princess|en|Princess|qid=Q863048|lt=Princess|1=Princess}}, {{ill|King|en|King|qid=Q12097|lt=King|1=King}} (in the imperial context), {{ill|Princess consort|en|Princess consort|qid=Q7406779|lt=Queen|1=Princess consort}}, and {{ill|Queen regnant|en|Queen regnant|qid=Q19643|lt=Queen|1=Queen regnant}} takes place in the imperial spirit hall, these ceremonies are carried out in accordance with the minor festival rites.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Matters Concerning the Rituals of the Imperial Household|ja|皇室ノ祭祀ニ関スル件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matters Concerning the Rituals of the Imperial Household|1=Matters Concerning the Rituals of the Imperial Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109595717}}
{{Law-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|皇室祭祀令|version=85868780|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:%E7%9A%87%E5%AE%A4%E7%A5%AD%E7%A5%80%E4%BB%A4]]
<!--Imperial Household Rituals Ordinance--><!--Imperial Household Rituals Ordinance--><!--Imperial Household Rituals Ordinance--><!--Imperial Household Rituals Ordinance--><!--Imperial Household Rituals Ordinance--><!--Imperial Household Rituals Ordinance--><!--Imperial Household Rituals Ordinance--><!--Imperial Household Rituals Ordinance--><!--Imperial Household Rituals Ordinance--><!--Imperial Household Rituals Ordinance--><!--Imperial Household Rituals Ordinance--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11581038}} [[Category:1908 establishments]] [[Category:1908 in Japan]] [[Category:1908 in law]] [[Category:1947 disestablishments]] [[Category:Imperial Household Ordinances]] [[Category:January]] [[Category:Laws Abolished in 1947]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:September 1908]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
